steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims congestion worsened during the 1990s in both large cities (Miami) and smaller areas (Allentown-Bethlehem, Boise), with smaller areas less severely affected but still trending upward. The correct answer must show Miami consistently higher than a smaller area, with both rising.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Miami's delays always exceeded Boise's from 1990 to 2000, and both rose': Miami goes from 31 to 49 hours; Boise from 16 to 27 hours — differential severity and shared upward trend confirmed. ✓
  • Choice B: Contradicts actual data values in the graph. ✗
  • Choice C: Contradicts actual data values in the graph. ✗
  • Choice D: Addresses only an incidental point rather than the sustained pattern the student describes. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
